Houzz Pro vs JobTread (2026)

Two affordable platforms for remodelers and small GCs — but they solve different problems. Here's how to choose.

✓ Updated April 2026

Short answer: Houzz Pro is better for design-forward remodeling contractors who care about lead generation, portfolio, and client experience. JobTread is better for contractors who want serious job costing and financial visibility at an affordable price.

Side-by-side comparison

Feature Houzz Pro JobTread
Starting price $65/mo (Starter) $99/mo (Basic)
Target market Remodelers, design-build, kitchen/bath Small-mid GCs and remodelers
Lead generation Yes — Houzz marketplace exposure No
Portfolio/profile Excellent — Houzz brand recognition Not included
Proposals Visually rich with product photos Clean, detailed line-item proposals
Job costing Basic Strong — real-time budget vs. actual
Scheduling Basic timeline view Gantt scheduling with dependencies
Client communication Excellent — design-integrated Good — clean client portal
Change orders Yes Yes — with job cost impact
Subcontractor tools Basic Good
QuickBooks integration Yes (QBO) Yes (QBO)
Mobile app Good Good

Houzz Pro: The design-forward remodeling platform

Pricing: $65/mo (Starter) · $149/mo (Essential) · $399/mo (Pro)

Houzz Pro's primary competitive advantage isn't its project management features — it's its connection to the Houzz marketplace. Contractors on Houzz Pro get a professional profile on one of the largest design and home improvement platforms in the US, with access to homeowners actively looking for contractors. For remodelers who struggle with lead generation, this alone can justify the subscription cost.

The software side of Houzz Pro is built for the design-forward client experience: visually rich proposals that embed product photos, mood boards, and design concepts; a client communication hub that keeps all project conversations in one place; and budget tracking tied to selections. Clients sign proposals and change orders digitally and have a portal to track progress.

Where Houzz Pro is lighter: it's not a serious job costing tool. Budget vs. actual tracking exists but isn't the platform's strength. If financial visibility is your primary management need, Houzz Pro won't give you the depth that JobTread provides.

Houzz Pro — Design-forward proposals and Houzz marketplace visibility for remodeling contractors.

Visit Houzz Pro →

JobTread: Serious job costing for smaller contractors

Pricing: $99/mo (Basic) · $199/mo (Standard) · $299–$499/mo (Advanced)

JobTread built its reputation on job costing done right for smaller contractors. Real-time budget vs. actual tracking, cost category breakdowns, and clear profitability visibility per job — at a price point that makes sense for businesses that aren't large enough to justify Buildertrend's $499+ monthly cost.

JobTread also handles the full project lifecycle: estimates, proposals, job scheduling, client communication, and QuickBooks integration. The interface is clean and the learning curve is reasonable. It's not as feature-rich as Buildertrend, but for contractors who want to understand their job profitability in real time, it delivers.

What JobTread doesn't have: lead generation, a design-forward proposal format, or integration with a homeowner-facing marketplace. If those things matter to you, Houzz Pro has an advantage.

Price comparison: Total cost of ownership

At entry level, Houzz Pro ($65/mo) is cheaper than JobTread ($99/mo). But the comparison shifts at higher tiers — Houzz Pro's top plan is $399/mo while JobTread's comparable tier is $299/mo. Both offer annual billing discounts.

Factor in the Houzz marketplace lead generation value when comparing — if Houzz Pro generates one additional job per month that you wouldn't have gotten otherwise, it can pay for itself several times over depending on your average project size.

Who should choose Houzz Pro

  • Kitchen and bath remodelers where design aesthetic is a selling point
  • Contractors who want leads from the Houzz marketplace
  • Design-build firms where the proposal needs to reflect your design brand
  • Remodelers who compete on client experience rather than lowest price

Who should choose JobTread

  • GCs and remodelers who want serious job cost tracking at accessible price points
  • Contractors who already have a lead generation strategy (referrals, Google ads, word of mouth)
  • Builders who find Buildertrend's price prohibitive but want its general approach
  • Companies where financial visibility is the primary software need

Frequently asked questions

Can I use Houzz Pro without the marketplace listing?

Yes — Houzz Pro's software features are available regardless of whether you actively use the Houzz marketplace for leads. But the marketplace integration is one of Houzz Pro's primary differentiators, so if you're not interested in it, make sure the software features alone justify the cost vs. alternatives.

Does JobTread work for GCs as well as remodelers?

Yes — JobTread is used by both GCs and remodeling contractors. It's particularly well-suited for smaller general contractors ($200K–$3M annual revenue) who do a mix of project types and want job costing that works across all of them.

Which is easier to learn?

Both are considered accessible platforms. JobTread has a reputation for a clean, intuitive interface that contractors with limited software experience can pick up quickly. Houzz Pro's design-focused tools are straightforward for the proposal and client communication features, though the full platform has a learning curve.
